questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "I do not really understand why people discourage us from drinking beer. Beer is good for health, a mood elevator. Obesity is a small price to pay for such a good thing." - A tells B.
Assumptions: I. Beer is not good for health.
II. Beer is good for health.
III. Apart from obesity, there are no disadvantages of drinking beer.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "I cannot get my daughter married to him. He drinks, gambles and even visits brothels." - A tells B.
Assumptions: I. My daughter will agree to my decision.
II. Drinking is not a desirable quality of a husband.
III. It is interested in marrying A's daughter.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "They shall be punished. I gave them several warnings but they did not stop wearing pleated skirts in school." - The principal tells a teacher.
Assumptions: I. The principal has the authority to punish them.
II. A punishment must always follow some warnings.
III. Pleated skirts are not allowed in the school.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "The Red Line buses must be banned. They have killed so many people on the roads." - A passenger tells a colleague.
Assumptions: I. The passenger has the authority to ban them.
II. Buses that kill must be banned.
III. The colleague may agree with the passenger's view.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "The death of the convict in the police custody was a result of excessive torture. His autopsy proves it." - A lawyer in the court
Assumptions: I. The convict died in police custody.
II. Autopsy can be used to find the reason of death.
III. The lawyer is an expert on autopsy.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "The Bombay Stock Exchange Index has collapsed. Perhaps the brokers were panicked rumours of at the finance minister's resignation." - a newspaper report
Assumptions: I. The cause of the collapse is not definitely known.
II. The finance minister is important in the eyes of the brokers.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "Nathuram Godse was prepared to kill anybody who came between him and his dream of a 'Hindu Rashtra'. He had his reasons to kill Gandhi." - a book of history
Assumptions: I. The author knows the character of Nathuram Godse well.
II. Godse thought that Gandhiji was an obstruction in the way to a 'Hindu Rashtra'.
III. Godse might be a fanatic but he was not irrational.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "There is disagreement on many questions. But the conflict with Chechnya can still be resolved by peaceful means." - Russian defence minister
Assumptions: I. Disagreement can be resolved by peaceful means.
II. Chechnya may be persuaded.
III. Chechnya may not be persuaded.

choices.d: Either II or III is implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "The court here is plays so fast that I really lost my game. Yzaga was at a disadvantage." - Sampras after beating Yzaga in a tennis match.
Assumptions: I. Tennis courts should be fast and not slow.
II. Tennis courts should be slow and not fast.
III. Yzaga is a better player at slower courts.

choices.c: Either I or II is implicit
choices.d: III and either I or II are implicit

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body:
Statement: "The success of a film does not change my behaviour. Why should I don't think I have become arrogant after Baazigar's success." - Shah Rukh Khan in an interview
Assumptions: I. Some stars do change after a successful film.
II. Baazigar is a successful film.
III. There is no reason why a star should change after a successful film.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Statement: "We cannot prevent the shock waves but we do compensate for them. Insure with Oriental Insurance — the company that offers most comprehensive insurance against earthquakes." - an advertisement
